idea-plugin.name: devradar
idea-plugin.description:
devradar shows which of your teammates currently have the same git repository open and are coding right now — and lets you chat with them 1-to-1 inside the IDE. Works in any IntelliJ-based IDE (Rider, IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, GoLand, WebStorm, and more), and alongside the matching VS Code extension and Visual Studio extension — your teammates can be on any of them and you'll all land in the same room.

Zero configuration: your identity comes from git (user.name / user.email), and the "room" is derived from the repository's remote URL — so everyone who opened the same repo is automatically in the same room. No login, no token, no setup.

What you get:
  • Status-bar widget with a live "N online" count; click it for a popup that lists every teammate and which file they're editing.
  • Chat tool window (View → Tool Windows → devradar Chat): pick a peer, send messages, with "last seen N minutes ago" for offline teammates and banners when the connection is down.
  • Balloon notifications when someone messages you — even when the chat tool window is closed — with an Open action that jumps straight into the conversation.
  • Settings at Tools → devradar: custom server URL, display-name override, and an optional team key for private rooms (only people who know the phrase can join).
Intentionally not included (this is presence + quick coordination, not a chat product): no read receipts, no typing indicators, no message persistence (offline messages aren't delivered), no group chats.

The presence server is open source (Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects): github.com/mertkont/devradar
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.2.3

  • Listing improvements: full feature description with chat, notifications, settings.
  • Added marketplace screenshots (tool window, conversation, balloon notification, status bar).
  • Added <change-notes> so this section actually shows up on the marketplace.
  • No code changes.

0.2.2

  • All UI strings translated to English (was Turkish) for global use.
  • No functional changes.

0.2.1

  • Chat panel parity with VS Code: warning banner explains why input is disabled when the peer is offline or disconnected; right-hand status label shows "last seen 5 min ago" instead of a bare "offline".
  • Status-bar popup now opens chat for offline peers too.

0.2.0

  • devradar Chat tool window (right-anchored): peer combo + transcript + Send button. Click any peer in the status-bar popup to open the panel pre-selected to them.
  • Balloon notifications surface incoming messages even before the tool window is opened.
  • New action: Tools → devradar: Open Chat.

0.1.3

  • Marketplace icon switched to a proper hand-coded SVG (JetBrains Marketplace requires SVG).

0.1.2

  • Fixed "stuck on connecting" hang after macOS lid close — added a 10s WebSocket connect timeout and a 15s overall future timeout so the reconnect chain can't stall silently.
  • Serialised all WebSocket sends through a per-service lock (Java's WebSocket API requires it).
  • Cleared the stale peers list on disconnect.
  • New Tools → devradar: Reconnect action.

0.1.1

  • Status-bar popup shows "last seen 5 min ago" for offline teammates.

0.1.0

  • Initial release. Status-bar widget with online count, click-popup listing peers and their current files. Zero configuration — identity from git, room from remote.origin.url.

action.description: Open the devradar chat tool window
